requirementCueLines: -10 credits of college history with 2.50 average.
-10 credits of composition or writing with a 2.00 in each course.
10 credits of approved courses in the Modern period.
10 credits of approved courses in the Pre-Modern period.

30 credits of upper division history coursework (300-400 level) completed in residence at the University of Washington. Transfer and extension credits are not classified as "in residence." HSTRY 388, HSTRY 494, and HSTRY 498 courses all count as part of upper division history coursework.
5 credits each in four of the following six areas of study:
Approved Elective Courses for the Major
Civics: 10 credits/2 courses
Degree Requirements
Diversity
Diversity Statement
Economics: 10 credits/2 courses
Geography: 5 credits/1 course
History Endorsement Requirements
History Endorsement Requirements | Department of History | University of Washington

History is a minimum requirements major. Students may declare the major as soon as they have met the prerequisites listed above. There is no application process. To declare the major, please make an appointment with History Undergraduate Advising .
History: 35 credits/7 courses
History: 50 credits/10 courses
HSTLAC 283; 385
HSTRY 388
HSTRY 494; 498
If you have questions about these requirements, please contact the History Undergraduate Advising Office .
In order for a course to count toward these prerequisite requirements, a grade of 2.5 or its equivalent must be recorded.
Major Requirements
Middle Eastern & African History (all HSTAFM courses and HSTAM 325)
One 5-credit HSTRY 388: Introduction to History (Junior Seminar) completed at the University of Washington.

One 5-credit HSTRY 494: Historiography OR HSTRY 498: Colloquium in History (Senior Seminar) completed at the University of Washington in which a major research paper or its equivalent will be written.
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Historical Methodologies
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Historical Methodologies/Research
One course (approx. 5 credits) in Historical Research
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of Colonial America and the Founding of the U.S.
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the Ancient World
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the Early Modern & Modern World
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the Medieval World through 1450
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of the United States in the Long Twentieth Century
One course (approx. 5 credits) in History of Washington State and the Pacific Northwest

parsedRequirementGroups.label: The choice of remaining elective History coursework (to reach a total of 60 credits) is entirely at the student's discretion. This coursework varies, however, depending on whether you have chosen to declare one of the History Thematic Majors . For the Thematic Majors, students must choose 25 elective credits from the approved course lists available by clicking here . Remaining elective credit to reach 60 total History credits is at the student's discretion.
